steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice most accurately compares the two discoveries. Both discoveries involve DNA mobility: McClintock found DNA segments that move within a genome; Lederberg found phage DNA that moves from virus into host cell. The correct answer must capture that shared element accurately.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Like McClintock's discovery, which revealed moving DNA segments within a cell's genome, Lederberg's discovery also involved DNA mobility, showing how phage DNA will enter a bacterial host cell and incorporate itself into the cell's genome.: correctly describes McClintock's moving DNA segments and shows Lederberg's phage DNA incorporation as a parallel. ✓
  • Choice B — Both McClintock and Lederberg were geneticists: McClintock found that phage DNA could be passed to bacterial host cells, and Lederberg identified DNA segments that can change position within genomes.: attributes McClintock's work to Lederberg and vice versa. ✗
  • Choice C — McClintock's and Lederberg's discoveries provided insight into how phage DNA uses the lysogenic cycle to change position within the genome of a bacterial host cell.: merges the two findings into one, losing the comparison entirely. ✗
  • Choice D — While McClintock first discovered DNA segments capable of altering gene expression, Lederberg's discovery provided geneticists with a better understanding of how TEs move within host cells.: credits the wrong scientist with the wrong discovery. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
